text.title: Retention Metrics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Retention Metrics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each retention metric MUST have a single canonical SQL definition stored in a metrics-store. Recomputations in BI tools forbidden — must query metrics-store.

rationale.source: Mode / Looker LookML / dbt metrics-store practice

rule.rationale: Same name + different SQL across reports yields uncomparable numbers. Locked SQL = single source of truth for the entire org.

rule.statement: Cohort retention MUST include D1, D7, D30 (or 1d/7d/30d) at minimum. Each measures a different layer: activation (D1), habit (D7), retention (D30).

rationale.source: Andrew Chen retention curves; Mixpanel benchmarks

rule.rationale: Single retention number averages activation + habit + retention failures. D1/D7/D30 separates them; each has different intervention. D30 alone misses early-funnel breaks.

rule.statement: DAU/MAU MUST be reported with a numerator and denominator floor (e.g. exclude weeks with < 100 MAU) and segmented by plan / cohort.
rule.rationale: DAU/MAU in tiny populations is volatile and misleading. Floor + segmentation surfaces real engagement, not noise from small numbers.

rule.statement: Churn-risk score MUST be backtested on 6+ months of historical churners. Live-deployed without backtest is forbidden — produces noise.
rule.rationale: Untested scores produce high false positive rate; CS team ignores. Backtesting on real churners surfaces which signals actually predict and at what threshold.

rule.statement: Default retention reporting cadence MUST be monthly with quarterly deep-dives. Quarterly-only reporting hides month-by-month decay.
rule.rationale: Quarterly cadence misses early signals — by the time the quarter ends, 3 months of decay are baked in. Monthly catches inflection; quarterly contextualizes.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
